codeReview.summary: All 6 position/location stub functions are implemented with substantial test coverage (28 tests for initial commit + 21 edge fix tests). The implementations closely follow the Python references with correct data flow, error logging, and fragment store operations. Found 1 blocking bug and 2 non-blocking issues.

issues.description: CreateLocationMap conditionally includes $143 in location entries (only when EIDOffset != 0), but Python yj_position_location.py:1127 always includes it via `IonStruct(IS("$143"), loc.eid_offset)`. This means entries with offset=0 will be missing $143 entirely, which differs from the Python output. The fix is to always include "$143": loc.EIDOffset in the entry map.

haveContent merge logic does not concatenate text from merged chunks. Python lines 202-203 do: `text = (last_chunk.text or "") + (text or "")`, then passes the merged text to the new ContentChunk. The Go version creates a new ContentChunk without preserving Text from either the old or new chunk. This means merged chunks lose their text content.

CheckPositionAndLocationMaps is missing the section_lengths validation logic from Python lines 113-126. The Python computes section_lengths from map_pos_info, then checks reflow-section-size feature value against calculated value and logs warnings for mismatches. This validation is omitted entirely in the Go version. This is acceptable for M2 but should be tracked for future parity.

haveContent merge condition uses `lastChunk.ImageResource == eidSection[eid]` as the second disjunct for the OR, but Python's condition is `image_resource is None`. The Go code checks ImageResource against eidSection[eid] which is a section name string, not an image resource name — this is a logic error in the merge condition, though it may not trigger in practice since most chunks don't have ImageResource set.

haveContent merge skips allow_zero parameter that Python uses. After merging, Python checks `if length == 0 or not allow_zero: return` then sets `length = 0; text = image_resource = None` and falls through to the conditional template + final chunk append (creating a zero-length placeholder). The Go version just returns after merge when length==0, which means the function never appends a post-merge zero-length chunk with text=None, differing from Python behavior when allow_zero is False.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the go-port-worker skill procedure correctly — read Python reference first, implemented functions methodically, added tests, ran verification commands. Skill feedback confirms followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows systematic approach: read all context → read Python → read existing Go → run baseline tests → implement → add tests → verify.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The FragmentStore abstraction is a new pattern for managing fragments by type, separate from the existing fragmentCatalog. This pattern is used throughout the new code but isn't documented in AGENTS.md or library/.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: FragmentStore is defined in yj_position_location.go:280-325 and used by all 6 new functions. No documentation exists about when to use FragmentStore vs fragmentCatalog.

summary: Feature passes review with 1 blocking issue: CreateLocationMap omits $143 from entries with offset=0, differing from Python which always includes it. This will produce incorrect Ion output for location map fragments. Non-blocking issues include missing text concatenation in chunk merging, incomplete CheckPositionAndLocationMaps validation, and a logic error in the merge condition. All 11 VAL-M2-POSITION assertions have corresponding test coverage and tests pass. The handoff acknowledges some edge cases are simplified (footnote refs, $683 annotations, $749 external content, KIM conditional template reordering).
